channel.title: Acer Inc. — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Acer Inc. (acer).

item.title: CVE-2026-50226 · 6.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Firmware Theft & IMEI Spoofing via Connect-OTA
item.description: Fixed AES-128-CBC keys inside the AcerConnect OTA application let attackers forge authorization credentials for arbitrary IMEI numbers. This allows unauthorized actors to list catalog items and extract protected binaries from pre-signed cloud links.

item.title: CVE-2026-50225 · 8.8 HIGH 🌩️ — Account Creation Exhaustion
item.description: The registration path /v1/account/register provides no bot mitigation mechanisms, allowing malicious automated systems to flood the database.

item.title: CVE-2026-50224 · 6.9 MEDIUM — Unauthenticated IPv6 WAN Management Exposure
item.description: The web administration panel binds broadly to the public IPv6 address space on port [::]:8080 without default firewall limits, making internal API endpoints reachable over the WAN.

item.title: CVE-2026-50214 · 9.3 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Shared Secret Quota Inflation
item.description: The /v1/Plan service relies entirely on a shared global API token for full administrative management, allowing arbitrary creation of zero-cost network access plans.

item.title: CVE-2026-50213 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Bulk User Private Data Harvesting
item.description: The account validation endpoint /v1/User/validate returns comprehensive user profile data sheets, which can be crawled by iterating predictable identification strings.

item.title: CVE-2026-50212 · 7.1 HIGH — Arbitrary Remote Device Unbinding
item.description: Weak validation logic within device dissociation API routines allows a remote entity to forcefully unbind unrelated user endpoints, causing severe denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-50211 · 8.8 HIGH 🌩️ — Exposed Factory Testing App Boundaries
item.description: Leftover engineering diagnostics and factory-level diagnostic software remain exposed on retail builds, giving malicious apps write privileges to internal NVRAM registers.

item.title: CVE-2026-50210 · 6.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Weak Static Cryptographic Initialization Vectors
item.description: The device encrypts data using AES-CBC with static zero-filled Initialization Vectors (IVs), making it susceptible to replay attacks and known-plaintext decryption.

item.title: CVE-2026-50209 · 9.3 CRITICAL — MDM Server Registration Overriding
item.description: Broadcast events allow malicious software to rewrite the device's default Mobile Device Management (MDM) endpoint address, shifting administrative ownership to an external attacker.

item.title: CVE-2026-50208 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Permissive TrustAllCerts TLS Verification
item.description: High-risk TrustAllCerts routines disable standard TLS certificate validation. Combined with hard-coded DES symmetric encryption keys, a Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) actor could decrypt network traffic.

item.title: CVE-2026-50207 · 8.5 HIGH — Local Modem Manipulation via Binder Interfaces
item.description: The system Binder boundary accepts unverified pass-through AT commands, giving local applications the power to read baseband files or disable cellular connectivity.

item.title: CVE-2026-50206 · 8.5 HIGH — VPN Command Injection Vulnerability
item.description: Incoming VPN network profile settings fail to process special characters safely, enabling command injection via malicious config files.

item.title: CVE-2026-50205 · 8.8 HIGH 🌩️ — Plaintext Log Credential Leakage
item.description: System log files output unencrypted SMTP server authentication passwords alongside sensitive employee corporate identification data.

item.title: CVE-2026-49204 · 6.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Hard-coded AWS Cognito Testing Accounts
item.description: Leftover debug modules contain fixed credentials for internal AWS Cognito test sandboxes, risking asset exploitation.

item.title: CVE-2026-49202 · 8.8 HIGH 🌩️ — Unverified Meeting Recording Endpoints & Permissive CORS
item.description: Internal multimedia session archives are accessible without authentication, exacerbated by loose Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) rules that allow cross-site theft.

item.title: CVE-2026-49203 · 7.2 HIGH — Unauthenticated eSIM Configuration Manipulation
item.description: Crucial management API endpoints for cellular eSIM allocation do not validate caller authorization, allowing remote profiles to be rewritten or deleted.

item.title: CVE-2026-49194 · 9.4 CRITICAL — SCREEN_CLICK Authentication Bypass
item.description: The debugging routine SCREEN_CLICK(5053) enables a connection to skip the standard device login prompt entirely and directly enter an interactive shell interface.

item.title: CVE-2026-49193 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Publicly Readable AWS S3 Telemetry Buckets
item.description: Overly permissive configuration settings on cloud storage containers expose active telemetry information publicly to the internet.

item.title: CVE-2026-49192 · 5.3 MEDIUM — Summary Service Insecure Direct Object Reference
item.description: The summary service endpoint suffers from an IDOR vulnerability where it fails to verify user ownership of hardware serial numbers, exposing device data to scraping.

item.title: CVE-2026-49191 · 9.3 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Exposed Hard-coded M3WebServer Backend API Key
item.description: The production build of the M3WebServer hard-codes its backend API keys, which can be easily intercepted through verbose error handling pages.

item.title: CVE-2026-49190 · 9.4 CRITICAL — Missing Per-Instruction Authorization Checks
item.description: The system fails to evaluate instructional permissions over multiple internal operation codes (opcodes), permitting unauthorized application installations or command executions.

item.title: CVE-2026-49189 · 8.5 HIGH — Broadcast Receiver Privilege Escalation
item.description: Unchecked public access permissions on a core Broadcast Receiver allow unauthorized local software components to invoke administrative operations.

item.title: CVE-2026-49188 · 8.7 HIGH — Elevated Root Command Execution via ai_cmd Sockets
item.description: The ai_cmd utility executes with full root permissions. It pipes socket inputs directly to popen(), paving the way for unauthenticated users to execute arbitrary root commands.

item.title: CVE-2026-49187 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Hard-coded APK Resource Credentials & Scepters
item.description: The hard-coded APK resource files never expire, and the shared scepter leads to information leaks and potential misuse.

item.title: CVE-2026-49186 · 8.6 HIGH — Lack of MQTT Broker Topic Access Control Lists
item.description: The local MQTT broker does not enforce topic-level Access Control Lists (ACLs). This allows any client to subscribe using wildcard characters (# or +) to enumerate hidden network devices or publish rogue control commands.

item.title: CVE-2026-49185 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Instruction Injection via FieldX MDM
item.description: The FieldX MDM adb messaging topic passes unverified payloads directly into Runtime.exec(), allowing command/instruction injection.

item.title: CVE-2026-49201 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Acer Wave 7 router: Hardcoded Cryptographic Key
item.description: The upload.cgi binary, responsible for processing device backups, contains a hardcoded AES encryption key. This allows an attacker to decrypt, modify, and re-encrypt system backups, facilitating persistent backdoor injection.

item.title: CVE-2026-49200 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Acer Wave 7 router: Broken Access Control
item.description: The acer_cgi.log file in the device firmware is accessible without authentication via the web interface. This file contains cleartext login credentials (for web and Telnet), leading to unauthorized system access.

item.title: CVE-2026-49199 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Predator Connect W6x: RCE via MQTT
item.description: Crafted MQTT messages can trigger command injection, resulting in root-level code execution on the target device.

item.title: CVE-2026-49198 · 8.3 HIGH — Predator Connect W6x: MQTT Broker Access Control
item.description: Improper access control in the MQTT broker allows wildcard topic subscriptions, exposing all MQTT traffic to unauthorized actors.

item.title: CVE-2026-49197 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Predator Connect W6x: Improper Authentication
item.description: Web endpoints intended for the Acer Connect app improperly validate the HTTP Authorization header, failing to block requests when Base64 decoding fails.

item.title: CVE-2026-49196 · 8.6 HIGH — Predator Connect W6x: Web Interface Command Injection
item.description: The Wi-Fi device blocking feature fails to sanitize MAC address input, allowing injection and execution of arbitrary shell commands.

item.title: CVE-2026-49195 · 8.7 HIGH — Predator Connect W6x: unauthenticated Debug Service
item.description: Unauthenticated Debug Service. The /sbin/mtk_dut binary is exposed on TCP port 9000 without authentication, allowing any LAN-based attacker to execute arbitrary UCC commands.

item.title: CVE-2026-9789 · 8.5 HIGH — NitroSense V3: Security Vulnerability Information
item.description: A Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability affects Acer NitroSense software versions prior to 3.01.3052. The vulnerability stems from the the PSAdminAgent service, which creates a Named Pipe with a weak Access Control List (ACL). This allows an…

item.title: CVE-2026-9490 · 6.8 MEDIUM — Acer Care Center creates a Named Pipe with a weak Security Descriptor
item.description: A security vulnerability has been identified in Acer Care Center where the ACCSvc service creates a Named Pipe with a weak Security Descriptor. This vulnerability allows an authenticated local user to connect and send a specially crafted message (mes…

item.title: CVE-2026-9489 · 8.5 HIGH — NitroSense V3: Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability
item.description: NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program exposes a Windows Named Pipe that uses a custom protocol to invoke internal functions. However, this Named Pipe is misconfigured, allowing any authent…

item.title: CVE-2026-8069 · 8.5 HIGH — PredatorSense V3: Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability
item.description: PredatorSense version 3.00.3136 to 3.00.3196 contain Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program exposes a Windows Named Pipe that uses a custom protocol to invoke internal functions. However, this Named Pipe is misconfigured, allowing…
